The error indicates that PyTorch's default security setting (weights_only=True) doesn't allow loading the sklearn StandardScaler object that was saved in the checkpoint. This is because I saved both the model state dict and the scaler in the same checkpoint file. Analysis: To fix this, I need to modify how I save and load the model. Instead of saving the scaler in the same checkpoint file, I should either save them separately or use torch.serialization.add_safe_globals() to allow the StandardScaler class. The safest approach is to save the scaler separately as a pickle file or save its parameters (mean and scale) as numpy arrays. Decision: I'll modify the training script to save the scaler separately and update the prediction script to load it correctly.
